stage_title: Deepen Agenda as the model-handoff layer
stage_summary: Stage 6 deepens the implemented Agenda baseline into a real model-handoff layer. The repo already supports canonical task files, DAG files, task-log storage helpers, and the `azq agenda` command family for build, list, show, and dag operations. The forward work is commit-sized task proposals, review and acceptance flow, task dedupe, stronger dependency refinement, backend-neutral handoff packaging, execution reporting, provenance-preserving task lineage, and tighter use of task logs as durable evidence rather than dormant storage capability.
baseline_guardrails: Stage 1 Finis baseline is already implemented and remains the canonical source of accepted goals and lineage.
Stage 2 Formam baseline is already implemented and remains the canonical source of deliverables, maps, and accepted artifact definitions.
Stage 3 Agenda baseline is already implemented in canonical task, DAG, and task-log storage and must not be re-planned here.

Post-baseline Stage 6 deliverables must deepen Agenda task shaping and model handoff rather than recreate `azq agenda build`, canonical task storage, DAG storage, or basic lineage resolution from Stage 3.

Stage 6 must preserve Agenda's boundary: shape executable work packages and model handoff, without inventing a separate execution engine or collapsing review, proposal, and accepted task truth into one layer.

deliverables.title: Commit-sized task proposal and decomposition flow
deliverables.description: Replace the current stub-first task generation with a visible Agenda flow that decomposes one accepted deliverable into commit-sized proposed work units suitable for LLM-assisted implementation. Proposed tasks must remain distinct from accepted canonical tasks until a human review boundary is crossed.
deliverables.why_it_exists: The implementation plan makes commit-sized decomposition the first real Stage 6 target, while the current Agenda build path remains intentionally sparse. AZQ needs a proposal-bearing decomposition layer so executable work can be shaped without letting generated task text silently become accepted task truth.
acceptance_criteria: Agenda has a canonical proposal artifact format and home under `data/agenda/proposals/` that is distinct from accepted canonical task files under `data/agenda/tasks/`.
A live Agenda surface can generate more than placeholder tasks for one accepted deliverable when the deliverable warrants decomposition.
Proposed tasks preserve exact lineage to one deliverable and, when applicable, to the accepted Formam definition package that informed decomposition.
Accepted canonical tasks remain separate artifacts and are not overwritten by proposal text.
Tests or fixtures demonstrate deterministic proposal generation for stable input artifacts.
dependencies: baseline: Stage 3 Agenda canonical task storage

deliverables.title: Agenda task review and dedupe behavior
deliverables.description: Add a visible Agenda review and dedupe pass so repeated builds or closely related deliverables do not flood the task layer with redundant work units, and so proposed task text can be accepted, revised, or rejected before becoming canonical.
deliverables.why_it_exists: The plan explicitly calls out task dedupe, and the current repo has no real dedupe behavior beyond baseline exact-id storage rules. Agenda also needs a review boundary so generated task text does not leap directly from proposal into accepted task state.
acceptance_criteria: Agenda can detect and avoid duplicate or near-duplicate proposed task creation within the supported decomposition flow.
Agenda has a visible review artifact or command surface under `data/agenda/reviews/` that records accept, revise, reject, or merge decisions for proposed tasks.
Dedupe and review decisions are visible in task artifacts, review artifacts, or build output rather than hidden as silent data loss.
Canonical task identity remains stable when review or dedupe keeps an existing task instead of creating a new one.
Tests cover at least one repeated-build or overlapping-input scenario that would otherwise create duplicates.

deliverables.title: Deterministic dependency refinement and DAG integrity
deliverables.description: Deepen Agenda dependency refinement so task and DAG edges reflect a clearer execution order than the current sparse baseline while remaining deterministic, inspectable, and tied to accepted task truth rather than proposal churn.
deliverables.why_it_exists: The current repo already has canonical DAG artifacts and dependency fields, so there is baseline groundwork. The implementation plan names deterministic dependency refinement as the deeper next step.
acceptance_criteria: Agenda produces richer dependency edges when a deliverable requires ordered work, while preserving deterministic output for stable accepted task inputs.
Goal-level DAG artifacts stay canonical and inspectable under `data/agenda/dags/`.
Dependency refinement remains derived from accepted deliverable and accepted task truth on disk rather than opaque runtime state.
DAG refresh distinguishes accepted task structure from merely proposed task structure when both exist.
Tests demonstrate that refined edges remain stable across repeated runs.
dependencies: baseline: Stage 3 Agenda canonical DAG storage

deliverables.title: Accepted handoff package for backend-neutral LLM execution
deliverables.description: Create an Agenda-owned accepted handoff package that can prepare one or more reviewed tasks for Codex or another LLM backend without tying the repo to a single execution engine. The handoff package should act as the acceptance boundary between Agenda shaping and downstream model-facing execution.
deliverables.why_it_exists: The implementation plan calls for Codex preparation subsets and possible backend-neutral packaging, while the current repo stops at local task and DAG artifacts. AZQ needs a stable handoff boundary so downstream models consume accepted work packages instead of re-reading task proposal churn as if it were settled execution truth.
acceptance_criteria: Agenda can emit or maintain a stable handoff package artifact under `data/agenda/handoffs/` linked to exact accepted task identifiers.
The handoff package is created or marked ready only after task proposal, review, dedupe, and dependency surfaces have resolved into accepted task truth.
The package includes enough accepted task, dependency, deliverable, and lineage context to support downstream LLM execution without re-deriving basics from scratch.
The package distinguishes proposed task text, human revision text, accepted canonical task text, and execution-facing handoff text instead of flattening them into one bundle.
Tests or fixture-backed review confirm that the handoff package points back to exact task and deliverable identifiers and remains backend-neutral.

deliverables.title: Execution reporting and task-log integration
deliverables.description: Turn task logs from latent storage capability into a normal Agenda evidence surface, with visible execution reporting tied to accepted task state changes and handoff outcomes.
deliverables.why_it_exists: The current repo already includes `data/agenda/logs/` and log helpers, but the implementation plan and gap report both note that task-log creation is not part of the normal baseline flow. Stage 6 should make logs and reports part of the durable execution evidence chain.
acceptance_criteria: Agenda has a live surface that records accepted task execution events into canonical task logs as part of supported operator behavior.

Execution reports are written under `data/agenda/reports/` or equivalent canonical Agenda reporting storage and summarize what ran, what changed, and what remains without inventing a separate orchestration theater.
Task logs remain append-oriented durable evidence under `data/agenda/logs/`.
Execution reporting preserves exact lineage to the handoff package, accepted task ids, and resulting task state changes where applicable.
Tests demonstrate that execution reporting and task logs stay aligned with canonical task identifiers and status transitions.

deliverables.title: Provenance-preserving task and handoff lineage
deliverables.description: Add an Agenda provenance contract that preserves the boundary between accepted deliverable definition, model-generated task proposal text, human revision text, accepted canonical task text, dependency structure, and accepted handoff package content. This contract should make it possible to review how executable work changed without letting model suggestions masquerade as accepted execution truth.
deliverables.why_it_exists: AZQ is meant to be a wrapper that protects human intent from model drift. Stage 5 establishes that protection at the artifact-shaping layer, and Stage 6 must preserve it at the executable-work and handoff layer. The current repo has real task and DAG lineage, but it does not yet have an explicit contract for separating human-origin and model-origin shaping during deeper Agenda work.
acceptance_criteria: Agenda artifacts used in Stage 6 expose fields or sections that distinguish accepted deliverable definition, proposed task text, human revision text, accepted canonical task text, dependency structure, and accepted handoff package content where applicable.
The provenance contract preserves exact lineage to `FINIS_###` goal identifiers, canonical deliverable identifiers, and canonical task identifiers where applicable.

No Stage 6 artifact treats model-generated proposal text as accepted canonical task text, accepted dependency truth, or accepted handoff content without an explicit human acceptance step recorded in Agenda review artifacts.

Tests or review fixtures demonstrate one end-to-end example where accepted artifact definition, model-origin task suggestions, human revision, accepted task structure, and accepted handoff package remain visibly distinct across proposal, review, acceptance, and execution-preparation artifacts.
dependencies: baseline: Stage 3 Agenda canonical task storage
baseline: Stage 3 Agenda canonical DAG storage
